routing_rule._doc: v3 router. Three-way routing: (1) IF grep_tokens ≤ 120k, use grep (matches v2); (2) ELIF grep too large AND rtk-err-cat manifest row has metadata.rtk_input_truncated=False, use rtk-err-cat (NEW); (3) ELSE use tail (matches v2 fallback). Motivated by §3f's argocd finding that rtk-err-cat scored 0.56 Sonnet vs hybrid-v2's tail at 0.12 on the 89k-line argocd log — but ONLY when rtk's input wasn't silently truncated. Codex 2026-05-08-#2 [high] surfaced that rtk truncates filter input at 10 MiB and the v2 design ignored this; v3 explicitly gates the intermediate on the truncation flag (which run_rtk_baseline.py now writes to manifest metadata). The 120k budget is unchanged from v2 — calibrated on eval_diagnosis_*.json outputs at 13-case state. Like v2, v3 is EVALUATION-TUNED (uses_diagnosis_eval=True) and the headline numbers should be read as 'v3 demonstrates the §3f hypothesis on the 17-case calibration + 6-case hold-out' rather than 'v3 generalizes blind' — separate v4 train/holdout would be the cleaner generalization test.

anti_leakage._doc: Same calibration-leakage posture as v2: the 120k threshold was originally selected from eval_diagnosis_*.json abstain-cliff observations. v3's intermediate-routing decision is gated on metadata.rtk_input_truncated, which is a deterministic property of the rtk-err-cat manifest row — not derived from any ground-truth-aware artifact. So v3's INCREMENTAL leakage over v2 is zero (same 120k threshold + a deterministic-flag gate). The CUMULATIVE leakage matches v2: declared True so the lock layer makes it visible. v4 / future work: tune both the budget and the rtk-input-cap threshold against a fresh train/holdout split.
